Phase-78.NAME: VPN Device Abuse Alert Inbox
Phase-78.objective: Create an explicit admin alert inbox for likely abused VPN device configs, with device-scoped review, rotate, block, resolve, and archive workflows.

scope.in-scope: Durable VPN abuse alert table, backend alert service, admin APIs, compact admin UI inbox/archive, explicit confirmation actions, audit-safe action details, and verification smokes.
scope.out-of-scope: Changing install/deploy scripts, enabling ANTI_ABUSE_MODE=auto_rotate by default, blocking whole accounts, changing VPN config format, changing MTProto abuse alerts, or collecting new VPN IP history beyond existing endpoint context.

requirements.requirement-1: Admin must see open VPN abuse alerts that clearly name the user, device, config version, signal, and last observed context.
requirements.requirement-2: Admin must be able to view details, rotate the affected config, block the affected device, resolve without action, and browse an archive of resolved alerts.
requirements.requirement-3: Only confirmed ping-pong or multi-network abuse signals create alerts; normal mobile roaming must not create alert noise.
requirements.requirement-4: All enforcement remains confirmation-safe and device-scoped.

architecture.flow-2: M-081 creates or deduplicates VPNDeviceAbuseAlert when the event type is ping_pong_abuse_detected or multi_network_abuse_detected.
architecture.flow-3: M-006 exposes admin-only alert list/detail/action endpoints.
architecture.flow-4: M-076 renders compact open inbox and archive surfaces inside the unified Users admin inventory.

steps.step-1: Update M-081/V-M-081 contracts and index links for the VPN device abuse alert inbox.
steps.step-2: Add an additive Alembic migration for vpn_device_abuse_alerts with status, dedupe key, source event, user, device, severity, occurrence, and resolution fields.
steps.step-3: Implement backend/app/vpn/abuse_alerts.py service for create, dedupe, list, detail, resolve, rotate, and block workflows.
steps.step-4: Connect alert creation to confirmed M-031/M-025 anti-abuse events without changing observe-mode defaults.
steps.step-5: Add admin API endpoints under /api/v1/admin/vpn/abuse/alerts with redaction and explicit confirmation bodies.
steps.step-6: Write admin audit entries for resolve, rotate, and block actions without storing secrets or full config text.
steps.step-7: Extend frontend-admin API/types and Users page with compact VPN Abuse inbox/archive, open count, detail drawer, and action buttons.
steps.step-8: Ensure action confirmations state that only the selected device is affected and then refresh the unified users/device inventory.
steps.step-9: Add backend tests and Phase-78 static/admin smoke for inbox, archive, dedupe, no-roaming-alert, confirmation, and redaction gates.
steps.step-10: Run V-M-081 verification, XML parse, MyGRACE lint, git diff check, and protected deploy/install/runtime guard.

mental-walkthroughs.walkthrough-1: Normal roaming: endpoint changes once from Wi-Fi to LTE, M-031 classifies roaming, no M-081 alert row is created, admin inbox stays quiet.
mental-walkthroughs.walkthrough-2: Shared config: endpoint alternates A/B/A/B, M-031 records ping_pong_abuse_detected, M-081 creates one open alert, repeated scans dedupe into occurrence_count.
mental-walkthroughs.walkthrough-3: Admin response: admin opens the alert, rotates device config with confirmation, M-021 reprovisions only that device, M-081 resolves alert into archive with audit-safe action_result.

risks.risk-1: false-positive mobile-network behavior could create operator noise; mitigate by using only confirmed anti-abuse event types and no automatic enforcement.
risks.risk-2: Admin action could accidentally affect a whole account; mitigate by routing all actions through existing one-device M-024/M-021 boundaries.
risks.risk-3: Alert payload could leak VPN secrets; mitigate with explicit redaction tests and payload assertions.

acceptance.item-1: Admin can see open VPN abuse alerts and resolved archive entries in a compact interface.
acceptance.item-2: Rotate and block actions require confirmation and affect only the alert device.
acceptance.item-3: Normal roaming and Redis degraded cases do not create punitive or noisy alerts.
acceptance.item-4: Verification passes without deploy/install script changes.

Phase-78.evidence: PASS local: backend VPN abuse alert/admin/handshake tests 12/12 passed; frontend-admin build passed; scripts/phase78-vpn-device-abuse-alerts-smoke.mjs passed with protected deploy/runtime/user-frontend guard.
